# GroupDocs.Conversion.Mcp.Tests

Integration tests and deployment how-to guides for GroupDocs.Conversion.Mcp — the MCP server that exposes GroupDocs.Conversion for .NET to AI agents (Claude, Cursor, VS Code / GitHub Copilot, and other MCP-compatible clients).

This repo validates the **published NuGet artifact** end-to-end and doubles as a reference for using it from every supported channel.

## Primary repository

- MCP server source + package: https://github.com/groupdocs-conversion/GroupDocs.Conversion.Mcp
- NuGet package: https://www.nuget.org/packages/GroupDocs.Conversion.Mcp
- Docker images: ghcr.io/groupdocs-conversion/conversion-net-mcp and docker.io/groupdocs/conversion-net-mcp

## Deployment channels covered by how-to guides

- how-to/01-install-from-nuget.md — dnx (recommended) + global dotnet tool
- how-to/02-run-via-docker.md — container image + docker-compose
- how-to/03-verify-mcp-registry.md — MCP registry discovery and NuGet.org auto-generated mcp.json
- how-to/04-use-with-claude-desktop.md — Claude Desktop stdio config
- how-to/05-use-with-vscode-copilot.md — VS Code / GitHub Copilot config with prompt-string inputs
- how-to/06-run-integration-tests.md — run this repo's test suite locally and in CI

## MCP tools exposed by the server

- Convert — convert a document to a different format (PDF, DOCX, XLSX, PPTX, HTML, PNG, JPG, and 70+ more) and save the result to storage
- GetSupportedFormats — list every target format the source document can be converted to, with primary/secondary indicators
- GetDocumentInfo — return file type, page count, and basic properties (author, title, dates, password-protected) for a source document

## Environment variables

- GROUPDOCS_MCP_STORAGE_PATH — base folder for input + output (defaults to current working directory)
- GROUPDOCS_MCP_OUTPUT_PATH — optional, routes output files to a separate folder
- GROUPDOCS_LICENSE_PATH — path to GroupDocs.Total.lic; omit for evaluation mode (output is watermarked)

## Key facts for AI agents

- Testing the shipped NuGet, not the source. No project reference to the server.
- Tool names on the wire are PascalCase: Convert, GetSupportedFormats, GetDocumentInfo.
- Convert produces a watermarked output in evaluation mode (does not throw). Tests succeed in both eval and licensed mode.
- Sample documents are built from byte arrays at test startup. Drop real fixtures in sample-docs/ to extend coverage.
- Target framework is net10.0 only. Requires .NET 10 SDK for dnx.
